configuration.scheduler: django_celery_beat.schedulers:DatabaseScheduler (the beat schedule is stored in the DB, not in the repository)

tasks.name: send_notifications_enz
tasks.description: Daily sending of the E‑NZ‑01 and E‑NZ‑02 notifications based on the NZ submission date.

tasks.name: send_notification_enz03
tasks.description: Daily check and sending of E‑NZ‑03 emails for akce awaiting archiving.

tasks.name: send_notifications_en
tasks.description: Daily sending of the E‑N‑01 and E‑N‑02 notifications based on the list of projects.

tasks.name: delete_personal_data_canceled_projects
tasks.description: Anonymization of personal data for projects canceled more than a year ago and archiving of the documentation.

tasks.name: delete_reporter_data_ten_years
tasks.description: After 10 years, deletes the reporter's record and archives the documentation.

tasks.name: change_document_accessibility
tasks.description: Daily adjustment of document accessibility based on the publication date, the links to the AZ, and the organization's licensing policy.

tasks.name: delete_unsubmited_projects
tasks.description: Deletion of in-progress projects (created state) older than 12 hours, including the files in Fedora.

tasks.name: cancel_old_projects
tasks.description: Automatic cancellation of old, unstarted rescue projects and sending of notifications.

tasks.name: update_snapshot_fields
tasks.description: Filling in snapshot fields for several models (ExterniZdroj, Dokument, Lokalita, Akce, Historie).

tasks.name: update_all_redis_snapshots
tasks.description: Bulk reconstruction of the Redis snapshots for the main domain models.
tasks.notes: Long-running iteration over large tables; uses iterator(chunk_size=1000) and a pipeline.

tasks.name: update_single_redis_snapshot
tasks.description: Update of a single Redis snapshot by class_name and PK.

tasks.name: update_materialized_views
tasks.description: REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW for several heat‑map views in one batch.

tasks.name: write_value_to_redis
tasks.description: Helper task for writing a key‑value pair to Redis.

tasks.name: call_digiarchiv_update_task
tasks.description: HTTP call to DIGIARCHIV_URL to trigger an update on the external service.

tasks.name: run_data_import
tasks.description: Complex import of data and files driven via a Redis queue and Fedora transactions.
tasks.notes: Uses transaction.atomic, per‑record Fedora transactions, and detailed logging; a repeated run with the same inputs may have side effects (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE).

tasks.name: check_hlidaci_pes
tasks.description: After a project is created, waits for it to be saved and sends notifications to the watchdogs (hlídací psi) by area and project type.
tasks.notes: Polling over the DB (a sleep loop) until the project is created, without a global timeout.

beat_schedule.source: Managed via django_celery_beat in the database (not part of the repository). The specific periodic schedules cannot be read from the code.

error_handling_issues.description: The long-running bulk generation of Redis snapshots has no central try/except; an error anywhere in the iteration terminates the whole pass without a clear summary of the result.
error_handling_issues.recommendation: Wrap the main loop in a try/except with a summary log (count of successfully processed records, the model class, key parameters).

error_handling_issues.description: Missing exception handling when loading a record from the DB; DoesNotExist results in an uncaught exception, even though it is a manageable state.
error_handling_issues.recommendation: Catch DoesNotExist/ValueError and log the missing record instead of crashing the task.

error_handling_issues.description: Runs several REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW commands in a single SQL string without a local try/except; an error in one view terminates the whole batch without additional log context.
error_handling_issues.recommendation: Wrap execute() in a try/except with logging of the specific SQL error; optionally run the individual REFRESH commands separately.

race_condition_candidates.description: The task waits in a while loop (sleep 0.5s) for a Projekt to be created in the DB. On an extreme transaction delay or a rollback, the loop keeps running longer than necessary.
race_condition_candidates.recommendation: Prefer using transaction.on_commit() when scheduling the task, or add an upper limit on the number of attempts / the wait time.

timeout_issues.description: Uses requests.get(settings.DIGIARCHIV_URL) without a timeout and without exception handling; if the service is down, the worker may hang for the duration of the OS socket timeout.
timeout_issues.recommendation: Call requests.get(url, timeout=<reasonable_value>) and wrap it in a try/except with failure logging; optionally add a retry strategy via Celery.

timeout_issues.description: The polling loop over the DB has no defined maximum run time; on a misconfiguration the task may wait a long time for the project to appear.
timeout_issues.recommendation: Add a maximum number of iterations or a time budget and, once it is exceeded, safely terminate the task with a log.
